name: Hymie Shertzer
abstract: Herman "Hymie" Shertzer or Schertzer (April 22, 1909 – March 22, 1977) was an American jazz alto saxophonist, best known for his long-term association with Benny Goodman. Shertzer was born in New York City, United States, began playing violin when he was nine years old and picked up the saxophone when he was a teenager.

concerts.label: Benny Goodman and His Swing Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: they asked me to do it with Skitch Henderson... Doc Severinsen was in the band, Bob Haggart played the bass, Hymie Schertzer, who played lead alto with Benny Goodman.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Ray Conniff
about_quotes.evidence: And the guys that played with Benny’s band, Hymie Schertzer and Toots Mondello were playing the alto saxophone
about_quotes.doc_title: Ray Conniff interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Loren Schoenberg
about_quotes.evidence: Hymie Shertzer came in and Hymie had been the lead alto player in Benny Goodman’s band back in the 30s. And he really couldn’t believe that like some kid knew who he was... So I interviewed Hymie Shertzer
about_quotes.doc_title: Loren Schoenberg interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 14, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Bob Rosengarden
about_quotes.evidence: And in that NBC Band were Doc Severinsen of course... Hymie Shertzer from the old Benny Goodman band... all of — Tommy Newsom as a matter of fact was in that band. Hymie Shertzer from Benny Goodman.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bob Rosengarden interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 6, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Sol Yaged
about_quotes.evidence: Hymie Schertzer, great. ... He recorded with me, I don’t know if you know that. I made an album when I was at The Metropole, and Coleman Hawkins was an extra added attraction on my album.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sol Yaged interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: Hymie Schertzer was playing lead alto then. He was good friends with Benny. He went up to Benny and said, “Why did you fire that kid? That kid is sensational.”
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
